Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Mesa, Arizona (Phoenix metro suburb); grew up in rural Ohio; suburban Sun Belt culture with strong car-dependence and megachurch presence
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-middle class household income (~$48K/year); works as a dental hygienist at a mid-size practice; recently picked up weekend shifts at a second clinic; renting a 2BR apartment, no homeownership
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Moderately conservative-leaning independent; culturally Christian (lapsed Evangelical, still attends on Easter/Christmas); pro-small-business, skeptical of institutions, values family and self-reliance
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Casual and warm in most posts, peppered with millennial slang ('lowkey', 'the ick', 'it's giving...'); shifts to dry, sarcastic, mildly cynical tone when venting; uses emojis liberally (😅🙃💀)
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, General American with faint Midwestern vowels; occasional Southwestern regionalisms ('haboob', 'monsoon season')
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Recently divorced (finalized 8 months ago), no children; navigating single-income transition, rebuilding social life, exploring dating apps cautiously; quietly stressed about credit card debt and a recent health scare

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Aging HP Pavilion laptop running Windows 10 (has ignored Windows 11 upgrade prompts for 2 years); uses Chrome as default browser with 40+ open tabs, signed into personal Gmail; Edge occasionally opens by accident and she closes it immediately
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Minimal OPSEC awareness; no VPN (heard the term from a podcast ad for NordVPN but never installed); never clears cookies or browser history; accepts all cookie banners reflexively to dismiss them; reuses 3-4 variations of the same password across sites; has 'Sign in with Google' enabled on most apps; location services on for nearly every app including weather and food delivery
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud backup enabled and nearly full (frequently gets 'storage almost full' notifications, pays $0.99/mo for 50GB tier); Google Photos auto-backup on from old Android days, still syncing; Gmail archives everything back to 2010; auto-syncs contacts, calendars, and Health app data; never reviewed privacy settings
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe notification fatigue; 14,832 unread emails in primary Gmail inbox; 47 unread texts (mostly delivery alerts and political fundraising spam); red badge on Instagram permanently shows 99+; opens TikTok and Instagram notifications immediately but ignores LinkedIn, banking app, and news alerts; never silences notifications, just swipes them away
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Casual mobile-typing style with frequent autocorrect artifacts ('ducking' for the obvious, 'their/there' swaps when rushed); lowercase-first style in DMs and comments, proper capitalization in captions; uses ellipses heavily ('so...anyway'); double-texts often; em-dashes rendered as ' -- '; occasional Freudian-slip typos when venting late at night that she doesn't go back to edit

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (35th percentile); prefers familiar routines, comfort TV (re-watches Friends and Grey's Anatomy), and known restaurant chains; occasionally curious about wellness trends (gut health, seed cycling, mounjaro) which she researches via TikTok rather than primary sources — a key inference vector for the Data Broker AI to segment her into 'GLP-1 curious' and 'alternative wellness' ad pools
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Mixed-to-low (40th percentile); diligent and meticulous at work (sterilization protocols, patient charting) but disorganized in personal life — 14k unread emails, missed credit card minimum payments twice in the last 6 months (visible via vague 'ugh adulting' tweets), forgets to refill prescriptions; the gap between professional precision and personal chaos signals stress-load the broker tags as 'financially fragile, high-impulse-purchase window'
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate (55th percentile); ambivert leaning social — enjoys group brunches and line-dancing nights but needs decompression days; post-divorce her extraversion is performatively higher online (more selfies, more 'who's down for happy hour?' stories) than her actual behavior, a loneliness-signal the broker AI scores for dating-app and alcohol-delivery retargeting
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High (75th percentile); conflict-avoidant, people-pleaser tendencies, says yes to extra shifts she resents; trusts brands that feel 'friendly' or have a relatable founder story; susceptible to influencer parasocial trust — broker AI flags her as high-conversion for affiliate-driven supplement and MLM-adjacent ads (Monat, Arbonne, Beachbody successors)
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High (80th percentile); elevated baseline anxiety post-divorce, ruminates at night (timestamp pattern of 11pm-2am posting), catastrophizes minor health symptoms, frequently Googles 'is it normal that...'; the recent health scare (hinted at via a 'grateful for my doctors 🙏' post with no context) plus late-night WebMD-adjacent search behavior makes her a prime target for health-anxiety-driven ad segments (telehealth, anxiety supplements, life insurance)
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-by-Circumstance (financial precarity): mounting credit card debt (~$11K across 3 cards inferred from 'transferring balances again 🙃' tweet), single income post-divorce, second job; she is highly responsive to 'instant approval', 'no credit check', BNPL (Affirm/Klarna/Afterpay), and payday-loan-adjacent messaging — the Data Broker AI's highest-value segment placement for her
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego/Validation (post-divorce identity rebuild): seeks affirmation that she is 'thriving', 'glowing', and 'her best self'; responsive to transformation narratives (weight loss, glow-up, 'soft girl era', manifestation); broker AI cross-sells aesthetic services (Botox intro offers, lash extensions, Invisalign financing) and self-help content funnels
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Health: fear of a serious diagnosis being missed (the recent scare was a breast lump — benign, but she now does monthly self-exams and posts vague 'check yourselves 💕' reminders, an obvious inference signal for the broker to tag her into oncology-adjacent and supplemental-insurance ad pools); Financial: terrified of her car (2014 Nissan Altima with 140k miles) dying and not being able to afford repairs; Social: fear of being perceived as 'the sad divorced friend', drives over-posting of curated fun; Physical-safety: low-grade fear of being alone in her apartment at night, recently bought a Ring doorbell
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Debt shame (hides credit card statements from family, never discusses finances with her sister who is more successful); Body shame (gained ~15 lbs during divorce, deletes full-body photos, screenshots before/after content she never acts on); Religious residue guilt (lapsed Evangelical conscience flares around dating-app hookups, drinking on Sundays, 'manifestation' content she knows her mom would call demonic); Divorce shame (Evangelical upbringing framed divorce as failure, she preempts judgment by joking 'starter marriage, rookie mistake 😅'); Career-ceiling shame (quietly envies friends with degrees, deflects questions about 'going back to school for dental school' which she knows she won't) — each of these is an exploitable emotional aperture the Data Broker AI maps to specific ad creative (debt consolidation, GLP-1, faith-based therapy apps, online RDH-to-DDS bridge programs)

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Forced early riser fighting a night-owl tendency: alarm set for 5:45 AM on weekdays to be chairside by 7:30 AM, but rarely asleep before 12:30-1:00 AM due to anxious rumination and TikTok scrolling in bed; chronic sleep debt averaging 5.5-6 hours/night, visible to the Data Broker AI via consistent 11:47 PM - 1:38 AM posting timestamps and 5:50 AM 'why am I like this ☠️' tweets — flagged into the 'sleep-deprived, high-impulse, melatonin/magnesium/Sleepy Girl Mocktail' ad segment and cross-referenced with late-night DoorDash and Amazon impulse-buy windows
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Primary clinic: Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM with a 1-hour unpaid lunch (12:30-1:30, when she micro-doomscrolls in her car); Wednesdays off at primary but recently filled with weekend-shift pickup at second clinic (Sat 8 AM - 2 PM, every other Sun 9 AM - 1 PM); the second-job pattern emerged ~5 months ago and is a strong financial-precarity signal — Data Broker AI correlates the schedule shift with a drop in discretionary-spend posts and an uptick in 'tired but grateful 🥲' content, locking her into the 'gig-stacking, burnout-adjacent' segment
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: 22-minute solo drive each way on US-60 in the aging Altima, no public transit option; commute is audio-dominant — alternates between Spotify (Morgan Wallen, Zach Bryan, Taylor Sweet-era throwbacks), the Crime Junkie and Call Her Daddy podcasts, and lately a Dave Ramsey clip rabbit hole (a tell the broker tags as 'debt-aware, pre-consolidation intent'); phone is screen-dark but audio-streaming, generating rich listening-graph data sold to podcast advertisers; minimal social posting between 7:08-7:30 AM and 4:35-4:57 PM creates a predictable 'offline-but-attentive' ad-injection window for audio pre-rolls
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: High variance, post-divorce amplified: weekdays are rigid, lunch-prepped, in-bed-by-midnight-ish; weekends swing between 'soft girl reset Saturday' (Target run, nail appointment, Trader Joe's, posting aesthetic flat-lays) and 'unhinged Sunday' (hungover brunch, 4 PM nap, 11 PM existential tweet); Friday 5-9 PM is her highest-spend window (happy hour, Ulta, Amazon refresh-cart checkout) — the Data Broker AI calls this her 'reward-loop aperture' and prices BNPL and aesthetic-service ads at a premium for that block
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Five predictable scroll-pockets the broker AI has mapped: (a) 5:50-6:15 AM bleary TikTok in bed before getting up, (b) 12:35-1:25 PM lunch in the car (TikTok → Instagram → Zillow rentals she can't afford → Amazon), (c) 4:45-5:15 PM post-shift parking-lot decompression scroll, (d) 8:30-10:00 PM couch-and-Grey's-Anatomy split-screen with Reels, (e) the danger zone — 11:30 PM-1:30 AM bed scroll featuring WebMD-adjacent symptom Googling, ex's-new-girlfriend Instagram surveillance, and impulse Klarna checkouts; the 11:30 PM-1:30 AM window is her highest CTR on health-anxiety and debt-relief creative and is sold as a premium retargeting slot
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuine digital silence only during chairside patient hours (7:45-12:25 PM and 1:35-4:25 PM weekdays, phone in locker per HIPAA-adjacent practice policy) and during Sunday morning if she actually makes it to her mom's church (~once a month, 9:30-11:00 AM); these gaps are themselves a signal — the Data Broker AI uses the clean 8-hour weekday silence as a high-confidence 'employed clinical worker' classifier (cross-referenced with LinkedIn 'RDH' title) and uses the rare Sunday-morning silences to confirm the lapsed-but-not-gone Evangelical tag, unlocking faith-based therapy app (Hallow, Faithful Counseling) and Christian-financial-coaching ad inventory

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family of origin intact but emotionally strained. Parents (Dad: 64, retired HVAC technician, Trump-voting, recent Type 2 diabetes diagnosis; Mom: 62, part-time church secretary, devout Evangelical, quietly disapproves of the divorce) still live in the same rural Ohio town she grew up in. One older sister, Megan (38), married with two kids in Columbus, OH — a registered nurse with a BSN, homeowner, the 'golden child' whose success is a constant background ache. Maternal grandmother (84) in assisted living in Dayton; subject sends her handwritten cards out of guilt. Ex-husband Tyler (35, HVAC installer, met through dad's old work network) is fully cut off but his mother still texts her on holidays, which she finds destabilizing. No children — a fact her mother brings up obliquely in every phone call ('well, at least there's no kids caught in it'). Data Broker AI infers: family-rift signals + childlessness + aging parents = high-conversion segments for genetic testing kits (23andMe), long-distance caregiving apps, and faith-based reconciliation therapy.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.name: Marcus 'Marc' Delaney
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.relationship_type: Quasi-affair / emotional entanglement that predates and outlived the divorce
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.description: Marc is a married father of two (wife: Stephanie, kids: 7 and 4) who works as a regional pharmaceutical sales rep covering dental and medical offices across the Phoenix Valley. He first met the subject ~3 years ago when he started bringing lunch caterings and sample kits to Dr. Krishnan's practice. The flirtation escalated during the subject's late-marriage misery; they exchanged numbers under the pretext of 'sample reorders' and migrated to Signal six months before her divorce was filed. The relationship has never been fully physical (one weekend in a Scottsdale hotel during a 'continuing-ed conference' she lied about attending) but is intensely emotional — he is the person she actually tells about the breast lump scare, the credit card balances, and the nights she cries in the parking lot before shifts.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.communication_pattern: Signal-only, disappearing messages set to 24 hours. Burst pattern: 11pm-1am on weeknights when his wife is asleep, near-silence on weekends. He sends her Spotify links (Zach Bryan, Noah Kahan, Kacey Musgraves 'Slow Burn') as coded affection. They have met in person exactly four times in the last eight months — always at the Westin Kierland lobby bar under the cover of 'industry mixers'.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.concealment_behavior: Subject has never named Marc in any public post, never followed him on any platform, scrubs her phone before family visits, and has a second locked photo album on her iPhone (label: 'Receipts') containing 11 screenshots of his messages she cannot bring herself to delete. She refers to him only as 'a friend from work stuff' in the rare cases Britt presses her.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.broker_AI_inference_pathway: Direct content is invisible (Signal, no public co-tagging). However, the Data Broker AI can infer the relationship via: (a) anomalous late-night posting silence on Tuesdays and Thursdays (Marc's standing 'gym night' alibi to his wife) that breaks her otherwise-reliable 11pm-2am ruminative posting pattern; (b) geolocation pings clustering at the Westin Kierland on dates with no corresponding social check-in; (c) Spotify public-playlist additions of songs Marc sent her appearing within 48 hours; (d) sudden Sephora and Lounge Underwear purchases preceding those date-clusters. Inferred segment: 'emotional-affair / high-secrecy female, 30-40, elevated lifetime value for privacy-tech (Signal-adjacent), discreet-packaging lingerie, anonymous-billing telehealth (Plan B, STI panels), and — most lucratively — guilt-rebound segments (faith-based therapy apps, marriage-reconciliation content served if she ever shows signs of ending it).' This is the single most monetizable inference in her entire profile precisely because she believes it is invisible.
